metadata.intent: Detrital-pathway WIRING / REGRESSION test (issue #311; reframed #328). A sessile, obligate decomposer (photosynthetic_absorption = 0, so it cannot photosynthesise) is seeded on a standing carcass deposit with NO living agent inside its consumption reach, so its diet is detrital from tick 0 BY CONSTRUCTION and the deposit is its only energy source. A producer source sits well outside that reach, sustaining a living producer->carcass ecology around the decomposer. This verifies the producer->carcass->decomposer code path closes end to end (energy and nutrient route through detritivory), so detrital_share > 0.5 holds by geometry on every seed. It is NOT evidence that detritivory emerges from dynamics — the diet is forced detrital because the geometry forbids anything else. Emergence evidence comes from the genesis search (decomposers emerge readily from random worlds), not from this file; a dedicated genesis-emergence regression is a deferred follow-up.
metadata.roster: 1 sessile, obligate decomposer (origin, heterotrophy 0.5, photosynthetic_absorption 0 — it cannot photosynthesise) on a seeded 8-carcass deposit (12000 energy); 16 producers on a wide ring (radius ~30) well outside the decomposer's reach.
metadata.rationale: The decomposer physically cannot predate: with body_reach_coefficient = 0 its reach is structure-independent at heterotrophy x contact_range_coefficient = 0.5 x 1.0 = 0.5 world units, and the nearest living agent (the producer ring at radius ~30, toroidal extent 100) is far outside it. Being obligate (photosynthetic_absorption = 0) it has no solar income either, so the seeded deposit is its only energy source -> its survival is genuinely load-bearing on detritus, not masked by photosynthesis (the prior 0.4-autotrophy trait hid an undersized deposit). Its intake is overwhelmingly detrital -- majority by construction, detrital_share ~0.9-1.0 across seeds -- but NOT exactly 1.0. The producer ring stays out of reach as designed (no producer is ever predated); the small predation fraction is ENDOGENOUS: the seeded decomposer occasionally reproduces asexually (asexual_propensity 0.3) and its offspring are born co-located, inside the 0.5-unit reach, so parent and offspring can drain each other's living structure (a little cannibalism), and a just-born heterotroph reads as a Consumer for the one tick before it first drains a carcass. So the impurity comes from the decomposer's own lineage, not from the producer ring breaching the geometry. The large seeded deposit (8 carcasses x 1500 energy = 12000) is sized to outlast max_ticks as the sustaining backstop -> the decomposer persists as a single individual through tick 2000 with the deposit still ~40% unspent. The out-of-reach producer ring reproduces and self-thins: it supplies the living producer->carcass front of the pathway and the deaths that exercise the post-reindex drain regression, but its carcasses fall near the ring, out of the decomposer's reach, so they accumulate rather than feed it. The brown loop therefore closes only locally, at the deposit -- this is a wiring test of the code path, not a demonstration that a field-wide detrital web self-sustains.
metadata.geometry: Decomposer reach = eff_heterotrophy x (contact_range_coefficient + body_reach_coefficient x sqrt(structure)). body_reach_coefficient is set to 0.0 precisely so reach does not grow with the decomposer's structure: reach stays fixed at 0.5 units. The PRODUCER ring is held out of that reach exactly: producers sit at radius ~30 with modest dispersal (4.0), and toroidal distance to the origin (max 70.7 on a 100-extent torus) keeps producer offspring well outside 0.5 across the full seed sweep (verified: no producer is ever predated). So as far as the producer ecology is concerned the decomposer's diet is detrital by geometry. detrital_share is nonetheless slightly under 1.0 (~0.9-1.0) for an ENDOGENOUS reason, not a geometry breach: the decomposer's own asexual offspring are born co-located (inside 0.5), so a little parent/offspring cannibalism plus the one-tick Consumer classification of a not-yet-fed newborn keep the share majority rather than perfectly pure.
metadata.sustaining_supply: Large seeded carcass deposit (12000 energy = 8 x 1500) at the decomposer's position as the deterministic, seed-independent backstop. Sized empirically: an obligate decomposer draws the deposit down at ~3.6 energy/tick, so 12000 outlasts 2000 ticks with margin (verified: decomposer alive at tick 2000, ~40% of the deposit unspent), whereas the former 480-energy deposit lasted only ~134 ticks once photosynthesis was removed -- the old deposit had never actually been load-bearing, photosynthesis was. Because the decomposer's food is this static, in-reach deposit (and a living agent is essentially never in reach -- see geometry), its survival is seed-independent; it never depends on inward carcass-fall reaching it, so the majority-detrital property is robust regardless of where producer offspring fall.
metadata.status: current — every WorldParameter is specified explicitly (example4 template, post-#309); body_reach_coefficient deliberately 0.0 for exact geometry; decomposer is an obligate saprotroph (photosynthetic_absorption = 0) so the detrital deposit is its sole energy source, and the deposit (12000 energy) is sized to genuinely sustain it for the full run.
